core_mechanism: A self-terminating supercritical multiplication (branching) process: a quantity grows exponentially once a control parameter crosses a critical threshold, and the runaway is quenched by a negative feedback (expansion driven by the accumulated output itself) that returns the system to criticality, so the closed-form total 'yield' is the time-integral of the exponential growth up to that self-limiting shutoff.
brief_reason: The abstract centers on a closed-form formula estimating the total explosive yield of a fission chain reaction, which is exactly a supercritical exponential multiplication process self-quenched by expansion feedback and integrated to shutoff, with the paper re-deriving that same integrated-yield expression from an alternative approach.
